response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar on paper — a technical bachelor's degree and roughly 17+ years of software engineering across web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems — but the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows a strong senior/staff-level hands-on engineer rather than a recognized AI leader. AI exposure is limited to recent MCP/agent tooling work, there is no advanced AI-related degree, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, and no evidence of advising executives or strategic customers, which are core to this role.
strengths: 17+ years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform used in production by NGOs
Strong craftsmanship and knowledge-sharing culture: TDD, open-source libraries, conference talks, and workshops
concerns: Seniority gap: career history tops out at senior/staff level with no principal, director, or org-wide leadership scope
No recognized AI expertise — no AI-related advanced degree, publications, or demonstrated AI products beyond recent MCP tooling
Limited evidence of strategic advisory work with customers, partners, or executive stakeholders

key_factors.factor: Missing recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority mismatch for director-level strategy role
Meets minimum experience and entrepreneurial background
